rulings.Case Title: DOROTHY RIEDEL ET AL VS. JOHN MUNGUIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys? Fees And Costs Incurred To Enforce Judgment [Welf. & Inst. Code 15657.5, Subd. (A); Ccp 685.040, 685.070]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, December 2, 2024, Line 2, 2- DEFENDANT JOHN MUNGUIA's Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys' Fees And Costs Incurred To Enforce Judgment [Welf. & Inst. Code 15657.5, Subd. (A); CCP 685.040, 685.070]. Defendant's motion for attorney's fees and costs is granted in large part. Defendant is awarded $68,292.25 in fees and costs. The motion is timely. Defendant previously calendared this motion and, by minute order of November 12, 2024, the court expressly allowed defendant to re-calendar the motion on a date after November 28, 2024. In any event, because plaintiff addressed the merit, she waived any notice defect. (See Carlton v. Quint (2000) 77 Cal.App.4th 690, 697 [party may waive notice defect by addressing the merits].) Defendant is entitled to fees for prevailing in this action and for its collection efforts. (See Welfare & Institutions Code section 15657.5(a); CCP 685.040; 685.070.) The court employes the lodestar method to determine reasonable fees. (See Lealao v. Beneficial California Inc. (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 19, 26 [number of hours times reasonable rate].) Counsel's billable hourly rate of $395/hour is reasonable. Defendant claims $72,637.25 and cites counsel's billing records. (McCann, Ex. 8.) The court strikes $1580 from the requested sum because the reference to the Wong trial was an error (McCann, Ex. 8 [1/16/24 entry]). Defendant concedes the mistake. (Reply, 4:10-11.) The court further strikes $2765 for defendant's ex parte request to appoint an elisor. Defendant should have known that the request to appoint an elisor would be contested and was not a proper ex parte application. All other requested fees and costs are proper and there is no basis for a negative multiplier. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DOROTHY RIEDEL ET AL VS. JOHN MUNGUIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Appointing Elisor [Ccp 128(A)(4), 177]; Memorandum
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, December 2, 2024, Line 2, 1- DEFENDANT JOHN MUNGUIA's Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Appointing Elisor [CCP 128(A)(4), 177]. Defendant's motion to appoint an elisor is granted. The motion is timely. Defendant previously calendared this motion and, by minute order of November 12, 2024, the court expressly allowed defendant to re-calendar the motion on a date after November 28, 2024. In any event, because plaintiff addressed the merits, she waived any notice defect. (See Carlton v. Quint (2000) 77 Cal.App.4th 690, 697 [party may waive notice defect by addressing the merits].) Per the inherent powers of the court, there is good cause to appoint an elisor to effectuate this court's prior orders (including the 9/12/24 assignment order) and judgment. (CCP 128(a)(8); CCP 177; Blueberry Properties, LLC v. Chow (2014) 230 Cal.App.4th 1017, 1020 ["A court typically appoints an elisor to sign documents on behalf of a recalcitrant party in order to effectuate its judgments or orders, where the party refuses to execute such documents."].) Plaintiff has demonstrated a pattern of recalcitrance and stymied defendant's collection efforts. The court does not entertain plaintiff's arguments regarding the propriety of the assignment order because: (1) many, if not all, of those arguments regarding exemptions and other matters were never previously raised and ostensibly waived (see 8/26/24 opposition to assignment); (2) re-visiting that order would be an improper motion for reconsideration; and (3) plaintiff has appealed the order and may raise those issues with the Court of Appeal. The court denies plaintiff's request for a continuance due to the protracted history of this litigation. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KAPOLEI ENERGY STORAGE I, LLC VS. MOSS & ASSOCIATES, LLC,
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, December 2, 2024, Line 13, 2- DEFENDANT MOSS & ASSOCIATES, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendant Moss & Associates' anti-SLAPP motion to strike the second cause of action for breach of contract is denied. The filing of a mechanic's lien is not protected activity under the anti-SLAPP statute and, even if it were, plaintiff Kapolei Energy Storage I has shown that there is at least minimal merit to the cause of action sought to be stricken. rulings.Case Title: SHAWN NELSON BREWER VS. VITAWERKS, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, December 2, 2024, Line 5, DEFENDANT VITAWERKS, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS. Defendant VitaWerks, Inc.'s motion to quash service of summons is denied. Vitawerks' reliance on California Rule of Court 3.110(b) is misplaced. Subdivision (f) of that rule merely authorizes the court to "issue an order to show cause why sanctions shall not be imposed" where, as here, a party has not complied with the time frames established by the rule. VitaWerks' request for monetary sanctions in its reply brief also fails because no monetary sanctions were requested in its notice of motion and no authority authorizes the award of monetary sanctions under these circumstances. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)
